diagrams.title: System Bus Architecture Overview
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the three main components of the system bus (data bus, address bus, and control bus) and how they connect the CPU, memory, and I/O devices. It shows the bidirectional nature of data bus, unidirectional address bus, and various control signals like read, write, and interrupt. This foundational view helps students understand how components communicate within the computer system.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental structure of the system bus and how the three bus types work together to enable communication between CPU, memory, and peripheral devices.

diagrams.title: Bus Control Unit Internal Architecture
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the internal organization of the Bus Control Unit, including the Bus Arbiter (for priority resolution), Bus Controller (for generating control signals), Address Decoder (for selecting memory or I/O locations), and Bus Interface Unit. It demonstrates how these components coordinate to manage bus access, control data transfer, and handle conflicts between multiple masters.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the internal components of the bus control unit and their roles in managing bus operations, arbitration, and signal generation for efficient system communication.

diagrams.title: Bus Read/Write Operation Sequence
diagrams.description: This diagram depicts the timing sequence of bus read and write operations, showing how control signals (RD, WR, CS, ACK) change over time relative to address and data lines. It illustrates the address valid period, data transfer phase, wait states, and acknowledge signals. Understanding this sequence is crucial for grasping how the bus control unit synchronizes data transfers.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the timing and sequence of bus operations, demonstrating how control signals coordinate read/write operations and how wait states are inserted for slower devices.
